summary: Completed issue 115 locally. SpecBridge created a governed GitHub evidence-loop pilot using `issue-to-merge-github` dry-run output as the connector action envelope, recorded issue 115 intake evidence, documented pre-merge comparison against real GitHub lifecycle evidence, and preserved the dry-run local command boundary. PR, CI, merge, and issue closure evidence remain external gates until the pull request lifecycle completes.
validations: powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ./scripts/specbridge.ps1 issue-to-merge-github -TaskId issue-115-github-evidence-loop -Title "Pilot issue-to-merge GitHub evidence loop" -Goal "Run a small end-to-end issue-to-merge task using the dry-run GitHub mutation envelope and compare it with governed real GitHub lifecycle evidence." -RelatedIssue https://github.com/yagooyarzabaldev-ops/specbridge/issues/115 -OutputPath .specbridge/issue-to-merge-runs/issue-115-github-evidence-loop.github-mutation-run.json -Force: passed
policy_result: Passed locally. The repository-local `issue-to-merge-github` command remained dry-run and performed no GitHub calls. Real GitHub actions are limited to governed issue intake, future PR creation, CI observation, policy-gated merge, issue closure, and repository memory evidence. No secrets, production, billing, auth or authorization security, database, dependency installation, CI/CD security, deployment automation, or production deployment paths were touched.
risk_result: Medium risk because the task compares a GitHub connector mutation envelope with real GitHub lifecycle evidence. Risk is bounded by dry-run local command behavior, explicit connector actions, repository-local evidence, security gate, review gate, ChatGPT/Codex audit, and GitHub CI as external authority.
merge_status: PR 116 merged on 2026-06-06 at https://github.com/yagooyarzabaldev-ops/specbridge/pull/116. GitHub CI passed. Issue 115 closed as completed.
